title: Deck The Halls

jokes.text: I get it. His hooves are full. He don't have no hands, I get it.
jokes.setup: Reindeer in children's story says he has his 'hooves full'
jokes.punchline: Will over-explains the obvious pun with characteristic street smart commentary
jokes.explanation: Classic Will Smith character moment - street smart teen explaining obvious things with confidence. Good character integration.

jokes.text: Hey, G, I did not see that coming. This is dope, right, Ash?
jokes.setup: Will acts impressed by an obvious children's story pun
jokes.punchline: Calls it 'dope' with genuine enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Will's street slang applied to innocent children's entertainment creates character-driven humor.

jokes.text: the new children on the block
jokes.setup: Vivian lists modern distractions including MTV, Nintendo
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces 'New Kids on the Block' as 'new children on the block'
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - sophisticated Aunt Viv's attempt to reference pop culture goes hilariously wrong.

jokes.text: Really? What a gyp.
jokes.setup: Vivian explains Santa gets the love of children as his gift
jokes.punchline: Will's materialistic dismissal of spiritual reward
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will response - cynical street wisdom applied to Christmas sentiment with great economy.

jokes.text: Wait a minute, there is no Milli Vanilli... but there definitely is a Santa Claus.
jokes.setup: Carlton says there's no Santa Claus
jokes.punchline: Will argues Milli Vanilli (lip-syncing scandal) is fake but Santa is real
jokes.explanation: Brilliant topical reference that perfectly captures Will's backwards logic and the Milli Vanilli scandal zeitgeist.

jokes.text: How do you spell 'Vanessa Williams'?
jokes.setup: Will is writing letter to Santa
jokes.punchline: Asks Ashley to help spell celebrity name instead of toys
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's teenage priorities - asking Santa for celebrities rather than traditional gifts.

jokes.text: Really? I think it's very autodidactic.
jokes.setup: Will calls her vocabulary learning boring
jokes.punchline: Ashley uses big word 'autodidactic' to defend learning new words
jokes.explanation: Self-referential joke where Ashley demonstrates her vocabulary obsession by using fancy word meaning 'self-taught'.

jokes.text: I know the meaning of 'undulate.'
jokes.setup: Will says Ashley doesn't know the meaning of Christmas
jokes.punchline: Ashley deflects with random vocabulary word
jokes.explanation: Callback to Ashley's vocabulary learning - she misses the point about Christmas spirit.

jokes.text: Give you $1 if you shave your head.
jokes.setup: Carlton needs to raise $600 for ski trip
jokes.punchline: Will offers tiny amount for drastic action
jokes.explanation: Classic Will cheapness - offering minimal payment for Carlton's embarrassment.

jokes.text: Imitating Jerry Lewis?
jokes.setup: Carlton says on December 26 they'll all be doing something
jokes.punchline: Will assumes it's Jerry Lewis impersonation
jokes.explanation: Random pop culture reference that plays on Carlton's dorky enthusiasms.

jokes.text: What the bloody hell kind of song is that?
jokes.setup: Glee club performs modern version of Christmas song
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's shocked British reaction
jokes.explanation: Geoffrey's proper British sensibilities clash with modern Christmas music.

jokes.text: A little kid sees his mom tongue-wrestling with Santa Claus?
jokes.setup: Discussion of inappropriate Christmas song lyrics
jokes.punchline: Will's crude description of 'I Saw Mommy Kissing Santa Claus'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reframing of innocent Christmas song with adult perspective. 'Tongue-wrestling' is perfectly crude Will language.

jokes.text: You dress up like the Easter Bunny. That makes me sick.
jokes.setup: Will misunderstands the Santa costume explanation
jokes.punchline: Jumps to Easter Bunny roleplay with disgust
jokes.explanation: Will's mind goes to inappropriate places with the costume concept.

jokes.text: Who's coming down the chimney, Barbra Streisand?
jokes.setup: Glee club program is all Barbra Streisand songs for Christmas
jokes.punchline: Will points out the mismatch with Christmas theme
jokes.explanation: Perfect observation about inappropriate song choices. Great visual of Barbra Streisand as Santa.

jokes.text: You've been to my parents' parties?
jokes.setup: Will accurately describes boring party music
jokes.punchline: Jonathan assumes Will knows from experience
jokes.explanation: Simple recognition that Will correctly identified stereotypical wealthy party music.

jokes.text: should I send the box of pears or the meat thermometer to Steve Guttenberg?
jokes.setup: Hilary sends gifts to A-list celebrities
jokes.punchline: Steve Guttenberg gets lesser gifts - pears or meat thermometer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary delusion - even her fake celebrity hierarchy has B-list treatment.

jokes.text: You don't know nobody.
jokes.setup: Hilary claims to know celebrities
jokes.punchline: Will's blunt street assessment with double negative
jokes.explanation: Classic Will directness cutting through Hilary's delusions. Grammar adds character authenticity.

jokes.text: You remind me so much of Judd Nelson.
jokes.setup: Will calls her jaded and skeptical
jokes.punchline: Hilary pretends to know another celebrity as comparison
jokes.explanation: Hilary can't help but name-drop even when being criticized - perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: The correct answer is Heather, formerly the muffin-cart girl.
jokes.setup: Another wrong wife name from Vivian
jokes.punchline: New wife was the office muffin vendor
jokes.explanation: Escalates the pattern - wealthy lawyers marrying service workers. 'Muffin-cart girl' is specific and funny.

jokes.text: Let's put it this way, Vivian. You're the only repeat.
jokes.setup: Vivian asks how many new wives will be at party
jokes.punchline: Philip indicates all other partners have new wives
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the setup. 'You're the only repeat' is brilliantly economical and dark.

jokes.text: Then at the end of the evening, the traditional police sirens.
jokes.setup: Will describes Christmas sounds in Philly - bells, singing, laughing
jokes.punchline: Ends with police sirens as 'traditional'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will perspective - romanticizing rough Philly neighborhood while acknowledging reality.

jokes.text: President Reagan's Secret Service agents dressed up as Santa and warned everyone to keep their distance.
jokes.setup: Will asks if neighborhood does anything special for Christmas
jokes.punchline: Bel-Air's 'festive' moment involves armed Santa impersonators
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist image of Secret Service Santas. Perfect commentary on wealthy neighborhood's disconnect from normal Christmas.

jokes.text: there's no winos making snow angels on the front lawn.
jokes.setup: Will lists what real Christmas should have
jokes.punchline: Includes homeless people in snow as positive Christmas memory
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that reveals Will's different perspective on what makes Christmas authentic and joyful.

jokes.text: That little disk?
jokes.setup: Vivian points out baby Jesus in crystal nativity
jokes.punchline: Will sees expensive crystal Jesus as tiny, impersonal disk
jokes.explanation: Great contrast between expensive 'beautiful' decoration and its actual appearance. Will's blunt assessment cuts through pretension.

jokes.text: Do they open your presents and wear your sweaters, too?
jokes.setup: Vivian explains decorating service sets up tree
jokes.punchline: Will extends the concept to absurd extreme
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation joke that highlights the absurdity of outsourcing Christmas traditions.

jokes.text: I hope they bring their Barbie dolls.
jokes.setup: Discussion of meeting all the new young wives
jokes.punchline: Vivian's cutting comment about their age
jokes.explanation: Vivian's rare sarcastic moment, perfectly timed after the new wives setup.

jokes.text: Will, were you vociferating for me?
jokes.setup: Will calls for Ashley
jokes.punchline: Ashley uses fancy word 'vociferating' instead of 'calling'
jokes.explanation: Another perfect Ashley vocabulary moment - using big words in everyday situations.

jokes.text: I got to get back to you on that.
jokes.setup: Ashley uses word 'vociferating'
jokes.punchline: Will admits he doesn't know what it means
jokes.explanation: Will's honest admission of not knowing fancy words - good character consistency.

jokes.text: They're beyond outstanding, they're deeply beyond.
jokes.setup: Shop worker describes Christmas figures
jokes.punchline: Pretentious art speak with meaningless superlatives
jokes.explanation: Perfect parody of pretentious Beverly Hills art speak - 'deeply beyond' is meaningless but sounds profound.

jokes.text: I feel it's a very vibrant tree, and I feel it's a very important tree.
jokes.setup: Describing Caribbean Christmas tree concept
jokes.punchline: Tree has feelings and importance beyond decoration
jokes.explanation: Continues the parody - giving emotional and social significance to a Christmas tree.

jokes.text: because the '90s are going to be all about restraint.
jokes.setup: Shop worker mentions saving money
jokes.punchline: Predicts '90s will be about restraint (while selling expensive decorations)
jokes.explanation: Ironic because it's coming from overpriced decorator, and retrospectively funny since the '90s were about excess.

jokes.text: with these little arrogant touches of celadon and periwinkle.
jokes.setup: Describing wreath color scheme
jokes.punchline: Colors described as 'arrogant' with fancy paint names
jokes.explanation: Colors can't be 'arrogant' - perfect absurdist description. Celadon and periwinkle are pretentious color names.

jokes.text: It's been done.
jokes.setup: Will asks for traditional red, green, Santa Claus decorations
jokes.punchline: Worker dismisses traditional Christmas as passé
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan dismissal of Christmas itself. Ultimate pretentious response - even Santa is too mainstream.

jokes.text: It's going to be about arrogant little elves and rambunctious reindeer... and little men in red suits. I think it will be deeply, deeply dope.
jokes.setup: Will mimics the decorator's pretentious language
jokes.punchline: Describes traditional Christmas in art-speak, ends with 'deeply dope'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback where Will uses the decorator's language against him, mixing high art speak with street slang perfectly.

jokes.text: Ashley wearing Will's backwards cap while surrounded by tacky Christmas decorations
jokes.setup: Parents return home expecting elegant decorations
jokes.punchline: Ashley has transformed into Will's style with loud decorations
jokes.explanation: Visual payoff showing Will's influence on Ashley - both in style and Christmas spirit.

jokes.text: So me and Ashley went down to the Pick 'n' Grab.
jokes.setup: Will explains what happened at Christmas shop
jokes.punchline: Instead went to discount convenience store for decorations
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast - from upscale Beverly Hills decorator to corner store. Shows Will's practical approach.

jokes.text: The blinking Frosty the Snowman is riling your attack dogs.
jokes.setup: Philip takes angry neighbor call
jokes.punchline: Neighbor's guard dogs are upset by Christmas decorations
jokes.explanation: Absurd complaint - even the dogs are offended by the decorations. Good Bel-Air neighbor parody.

jokes.text: Brother, you got a problem with my lights?
jokes.setup: Another angry neighbor calls
jokes.punchline: Will answers phone with street attitude
jokes.explanation: Will brings Philly street attitude to Bel-Air neighbor disputes - perfect culture clash.

jokes.text: Ain't no thing but a chicken wing.
jokes.setup: Continuing the phone confrontation
jokes.punchline: Another street phrase with food rhyme
jokes.explanation: Classic Will street wisdom - rhyming dismissal that's both tough and silly.

jokes.text: They are threatening to burn Frosty in effigy.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey reports on angry neighbors outside
jokes.punchline: Neighbors want to symbolically execute inflatable snowman
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - treating Christmas decoration like political protest. 'Burn in effigy' is perfectly pretentious language for the situation.

jokes.text: Still, I think it's best we avoid eye contact for the rest of the evening.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey helped with the controversial decorating
jokes.punchline: Wants to avoid being associated with the chaos
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey - proper but complicit, wants to distance himself from the mess he helped create.

jokes.text: There's only one way to do that. I think we should write a few checks.
jokes.setup: Carlton suggests solving neighbor dispute
jokes.punchline: His solution is throwing money at the problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton response - wealthy kid's first instinct is always to buy his way out of problems.

jokes.text: I'm afraid potential buyers aren't too crazy about the Kmart look.
jokes.setup: Neighbor explains property value concerns
jokes.punchline: Dismisses decorations as cheap store aesthetic
jokes.explanation: Perfect snobbish neighbor response - reducing Christmas joy to retail class distinctions.

jokes.text: Are they very angry with you over something?
jokes.setup: Neighbor asks why daughter and nephew decorated themselves
jokes.punchline: Assumes it must be punishment or revenge
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - assumes no one would choose tacky decorations unless forced or angry.

jokes.text: This reminds me of the summer my daughter Amanda turned 15... and tried to burn our house down.
jokes.setup: Neighbor compares decorations to teenage rebellion
jokes.punchline: Equates Christmas lights with attempted arson
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation that reveals wealthy family dysfunction - daughter's arson attempt mentioned casually.

jokes.text: I can tell by the sound of this guy's voice on the phone, I could take him in a minute.
jokes.setup: Will brags about handling the angry caller
jokes.punchline: Claims he can judge fighting ability by voice
jokes.explanation: Sets up the Evander Holyfield reveal - Will's overconfidence about to be destroyed.

jokes.text: Mr. Evander Holyfield. The undisputed Heavyweight Champion of the World.
jokes.setup: Will expects to easily handle angry caller
jokes.punchline: Caller is professional heavyweight boxer
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection payoff - ultimate subversion of Will's tough guy posturing. Geoffrey's formal announcement makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Will's physical reaction to seeing Evander Holyfield
jokes.setup: Will was confident about fighting the caller
jokes.punchline: Silent terror upon seeing who he challenged
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual payoff - Will's confident swagger instantly deflated. Physical comedy at its best.

jokes.text: You're the chump I talked to on the telephone. Phone? I ain't talked to you on no phone.
jokes.setup: Holyfield recognizes Will's voice
jokes.punchline: Will immediately denies phone conversation
jokes.explanation: Will's instant cowardice - goes from tough guy to denial in seconds. Great character reversal.

jokes.text: I'm not speaking to Bruce. I'll tell you about it later.
jokes.setup: Holyfield asks where Hilary was at Bruce Willis' party
jokes.punchline: Hilary has celebrity drama with Bruce Willis
jokes.explanation: Hilary actually DOES know celebrities - validates her earlier name-dropping while creating new absurd drama.

jokes.text: Frankly, I saw the lights and I thought they were... a little tacky.
jokes.setup: Holyfield seemed threatening on phone
jokes.punchline: Actually has refined aesthetic opinions about decorations
jokes.explanation: Subverts expectation - heavyweight boxer has sophisticated taste, agrees with snobbish neighbors.

jokes.text: usually this neighborhood is a complete dud.
jokes.setup: Kids choose best decorated house in neighborhood
jokes.punchline: Bel-Air is normally boring for Christmas
jokes.explanation: Confirms the earlier setup about Bel-Air's lack of Christmas spirit - validates Will's complaint.

jokes.text: Fight the power, Ashley.
jokes.setup: Ashley decides to keep the lights up
jokes.punchline: Will frames Christmas decorations as political rebellion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will - turns simple Christmas decorations into social justice cause with street activism language.

jokes.text: it makes me feel very ebullient.
jokes.setup: Ashley explains why she loves the decorations
jokes.punchline: Uses fancy word 'ebullient' to describe Christmas joy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ashley moment - even her Christmas joy is expressed with SAT vocabulary words.

jokes.text: Yeah, me, too.
jokes.setup: Ashley uses big word 'ebullient'
jokes.punchline: Will agrees without knowing what it means
jokes.explanation: Callback to Will's earlier admission - he still doesn't know what her fancy words mean but agrees anyway.

jokes.text: Thanks for the pretzels.
jokes.setup: Former President Reagan appears as neighbor
jokes.punchline: References Hilary's celebrity gift-giving from earlier
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback - Reagan actually received Hilary's chocolate pretzels, validating her celebrity connections.

jokes.text: because Nancy won't let me do anything fun to our house.
jokes.setup: Reagan compliments their Christmas decorations
jokes.punchline: Former President is henpecked by First Lady about decorating
jokes.explanation: Humanizes Reagan - even former Presidents have domestic disputes about Christmas decorations.

jokes.text: I was just saying they were absolutely magnificent, Mr. President.
jokes.setup: Neighbor was criticizing decorations moments before
jokes.punchline: Instantly reverses opinion when President approves
jokes.explanation: Perfect social climbing behavior - snobbish neighbor completely reverses position for presidential approval.

jokes.text: You know the one where it's all snowy... and the little jolly Santa is riding on a Norelco shaver?
jokes.setup: Will describes favorite Christmas commercial
jokes.punchline: Detailed description of specific vintage TV ad
jokes.explanation: Nostalgic reference to real classic Christmas commercial - shows Will's appreciation for simple pleasures.

jokes.text: with the horses with the furry feet are pulling the sleigh through the snow.
jokes.setup: Will continues describing favorite Christmas commercials
jokes.punchline: Describes Budweiser Clydesdale commercial with childlike wonder
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's simple joy in classic commercials - 'horses with furry feet' is endearing description.

jokes.text: He's on the shaver!
jokes.setup: Will has been waiting to see specific commercial
jokes.punchline: Excited exclamation when Norelco Santa appears
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff - Will's genuine childlike excitement over simple commercial. Shows character growth toward Christmas spirit.

tmdb_overview: Will inimitably dresses the mansion for the holidays, to the dismay of the neighbors, including heavyweight boxing champion Evander Holyfield.
